rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing semicolons and removing duplicate declarations
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types by aligning helper declarations with their expressions
Remove clauses with unresolved references to AADL properties or unit tokens
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types by aligning helper declarations with their expressions
Remove clauses with unresolved references to AADL properties or unit tokens
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use semicolons to terminate AGREE statements
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types by aligning helper declarations with their expressions
Remove clauses with unresolved references to AADL properties or unit tokens
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not add architecture to fix AGREE errors; instead, remove invalid clauses
Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Subcomponent ports should not be used in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
